name: Amiel Josh Basug
summary: I am Amiel, a Computer Engineering student focused on building reliable software, from embedded systems to full-stack applications and AI. I enjoy diving into Linux internals and self-hosted infrastructure.

education.details: Relevant Coursework: Data Structures & Algorithms, Software Design, Electronics & Electrical Circuits, Computer Network Security, Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning

projects.title: Arduino MAX7219 6-Digit Timer
projects.description: Designed a modular count-up/count-down timer using multiplexed 7-segment displays. Integrated debouncing logic, buzzer alerts, and optional LCD display support.

projects.title: Sentry - RFID Attendance System
projects.description: Built a desktop + hardware-integrated attendance system with real-time RFID logging. Implemented serial communication for automated Time-In/Time-Out tracking between Arduino and C# application.

projects.title: Home Server Stack
projects.description: Developed a self-hosted home server environment for local cloud services and system automation. Configured networking, service deployment, and system monitoring for personal infrastructure use.

projects.title: Orbital WiFi (Wireless Network Utility Tool)
projects.description: Developed a wireless network utility for scanning and analyzing nearby WiFi environments. Implemented low-level network interaction logic for signal detection and monitoring.

projects.title: Orbital Speedtest (Network Performance Analyzer)
projects.description: Built a network speed testing tool to measure bandwidth, latency, and connection stability. Automated repeated network performance tests for diagnostics and analysis.

projects.title: C++ Archives (DSA Practice Repository)
projects.description: Implemented data structures and algorithms for problem-solving and coding practice.

projects.title: Amiels Portfolio Website
projects.description: Designed and deployed a responsive personal portfolio showcasing projects and technical skills.
